title: Fix hardcoded trust score in content generation
description: Replace hardcoded trustScore = 50 with await getEffectiveTrustScore(userId) to enable actual CAPTCHA enforcement based on real user trust scores
current_code:
const trustScore = 50; // default; middleware could inject this via header in a future enhancement
if (requiresCaptcha(trustScore, identical.flagged)) {
required_change: const trustScore = await getEffectiveTrustScore(userId);
acceptance_criteria: WHEN user trust score < 40 THEN /api/content/generate requires CAPTCHA
WHEN user trust score >= 80 AND request is not identical THEN no CAPTCHA required
RPC call to getEffectiveTrustScore returns user's actual trust score from database
CAPTCHA enforcement is active and blocks abusers with low trust scores

title: Replace module-level Supabase singleton in wallet.ts with factory pattern
description: Convert from module-level singleton that creates service-role client at module load to factory function to prevent service-role key exposure in client bundles
acceptance_criteria: Module-level singleton replaced with adminClient() factory function
All references updated to call factory on demand
No module-level createClient() with service-role key exists
Wallet deduction and topup operations still function correctly
Middleware logs show no errors during wallet operations

title: Replace module-level Supabase singleton in generate.ts with factory pattern
description: Convert generate.ts from module-level singleton to factory function following the pattern from razorpay.ts
acceptance_criteria: Module-level singleton replaced with factory function
adminClient() called within generateWithDeduction function
No service-role key exposed at module level
Credit deduction RPC calls still work
Refund RPC calls still work

title: Replace module-level Supabase singleton in admin/auth.ts with factory pattern
description: Convert admin/auth.ts from module-level singleton to factory function
acceptance_criteria: Module-level singleton replaced with factory
requireAdmin function uses adminClient() factory
User account type lookup still works
Admin authorization checks function correctly

title: Fix IP extraction in middleware from x-forwarded-for header
description: Extract only the first IP from x-forwarded-for header to prevent rate limit bypass via proxy chains
current_code: const ip = req.headers.get('x-forwarded-for') ?? '127.0.0.1';
required_change: const ip = req.headers.get('x-forwarded-for')?.split(',')[0]?.trim() ?? '127.0.0.1';
acceptance_criteria: IP extraction uses .split(',')[0].trim() pattern
When x-forwarded-for is '1.2.3.4, 5.6.7.8', extracts '1.2.3.4'
x-client-ip header is set to only the first IP
Rate limit keys use the cleaned IP, not proxy chain

title: Remove PCI-sensitive data from webhook body logging
description: Replace body_preview in abuse_logs with SHA256 hash to prevent PCI/GDPR compliance violations
acceptance_criteria: body_preview removed from metadata
body_hash (SHA256) stored instead
No PCI-sensitive data (card, cvv) logged
abuse_logs table is now PCI-compliant
Test: trigger signature mismatch and verify only hash is logged

title: Reorder signup flow checks to validate device fingerprint BEFORE creating auth user
description: Move device fingerprint and VPN checks before createUser() to prevent orphaned Supabase auth users when device is blocked
acceptance_criteria: Device fingerprint check happens BEFORE createUser()
VPN detection happens BEFORE createUser()
When device is blocked, no auth user is created
Test: sign up with device that has 4+ accounts → should 403 before auth user created
Check Supabase auth user list → no orphaned users
All early validation checks (IP, email, existing account) happen first
roadmap_section: Phase 1, Day 2: Fix Subscription & Refund Logic

title: Insert subscription row into database after Razorpay creation
description: Add database insert to createSubscription so webhook handlers have a row to update when subscription activation event arrives
acceptance_criteria: After Razorpay subscription creation, a row is inserted into subscriptions table
Inserted row has status='pending'
razorpay_subscription_id is stored
Test: Create subscription → check subscriptions table has new row with status='pending'
Webhook handler can now successfully UPDATE the subscription row on activation
roadmap_section: Phase 1, Day 2: Fix Subscription & Refund Logic

title: Add error handling and alerting for silent credit refund failures
description: Wrap fn_refund_credits RPC in try/catch, log errors, and queue Inngest retry job to prevent silent failures
acceptance_criteria: Refund RPC wrapped in try/catch block
Error logged with userId, requestId, and error message
Inngest retry job queued on refund failure
Test: Simulate refund RPC failure → verify error logged with critical severity
Financial data is now tracked and visible
roadmap_section: Phase 1, Day 2: Fix Subscription & Refund Logic

title: Soften multi-account penalty for shared devices
description: Only penalize new user on multi-account device; only auto-block on extreme counts (10+)
acceptance_criteria: Only the NEW user is penalized (loses 25 trust points) on multi-account device
Existing users are NOT penalized when new user signs up
Auto-block threshold raised from 2 to 10 accounts
Test: 3 users on same device → only 3rd user penalized, not 1st two
Admin alert fired when 10+ accounts detected on same device
Family users no longer silently throttled
roadmap_section: Phase 1, Day 2: Fix Subscription & Refund Logic

title: Route content generation through lib/ai.ts abstraction with retry logic
description: Replace direct Anthropic client with createMessage() from lib/ai.ts to add retry logic and prompt caching on hot path
acceptance_criteria: Direct Anthropic client instantiation removed
createMessage from lib/ai.ts is used
Retry logic applied on transient API errors
Prompt caching is enabled if system context is provided
Test: Generate content → verify retry works (test with transient API error)
Token counting may need adjustment based on createMessage return format

title: Migrate middleware logging from console.log to structured logger
description: Replace raw console.log with project logger (pino) in middleware for consistent structured logging
current_code: console.log(JSON.stringify({ level: 'info', request_id: ... }));
acceptance_criteria: console.log removed from middleware
Project logger imported and used
Logs appear in structured JSON format matching pino
Test: make request → check logs in structured format
Logging is now consistent across the app

title: Add middleware sentinel for admin authorization re-validation
description: Add x-auth-verified header from middleware to prove token was validated, preventing header spoofing in admin routes
acceptance_criteria: Middleware sets x-auth-verified header after auth succeeds
requireAdmin checks for x-auth-verified='true'
Test: Attempt to spoof x-user-id header → requireAdmin rejects it
Admin routes cannot be bypassed via header spoofing
Only requests that went through middleware middleware pass the check

title: Fix IP signup limit race condition using atomic Upstash rate limiter
description: Replace manual Redis INCR/EXPIRE with Upstash Ratelimit.slidingWindow for atomic operations and prevent permanent IP blocks
acceptance_criteria: Manual Redis INCR/EXPIRE replaced with Upstash.slidingWindow
Operations are now atomic
Test: Max out IP at 3 signups → 4th blocked
Test: Wait 24h (mock time) → 5th succeeds
No risk of permanent IP blocks
Rate limiting is now reliable and testable

title: Extract auth cookie name to shared constant
description: Create SUPABASE_AUTH_COOKIE constant to prevent drift between middleware and other auth-checking code
current_code: req.cookies.get('__Secure-sb-access')?.value
acceptance_criteria: SUPABASE_AUTH_COOKIE constant defined in lib/auth.ts
Both middleware and other auth code use the same constant
No hardcoded cookie names exist in multiple files
Grep confirms '__Secure-sb-access' only in constant definition and test files

title: Verify or implement safe negative credit handling for refunds
description: Document that fn_credit_topup handles negative amounts safely, or create dedicated fn_credit_deduct_refund RPC
acceptance_criteria: Either fn_credit_topup is inspected and documented to handle negatives safely
Or new fn_credit_deduct_refund RPC is created with explicit deduction
Test: Process refund → user's balance decreases correctly
Test: Refund larger than balance → balance doesn't go below 0
Refund logic is now explicit and safe

Initial rates inserted (USD 1, INR 83, EUR 0.92)
getFxRates() fetches from database
priceFor() uses database rates
